7.reason: The loop condition assumes a newline is always present, but if the input does not contain a newline (e.g., EOF without newline), the loop will access memory beyond the string, causing a runtime error. Use s[i]!='\0' or i<strlen(s) instead.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  This loop condition assumes a newline exists; if not, it may read out-of-bounds, causing undefined behavior. Also, it should use i < strlen(s) to be safe.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition assumes a newline is always present, but if the input does not contain a newline (e.g., EOF without newline), the loop will access memory beyond the string, causing a runtime error. Use s[i]!='\0' or i<strlen(s) instead.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The loop condition only checks for '\n', but should also check for '\0' to prevent out-of-bounds access when the input lacks a newline. Furthermore, accessing s[i-1] and s[i+1] without bounds checking causes a runtime error if '-' is at the start or end of the string.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition checks for '\n', but the input string may not end with a newline character (e.g., when reading from a file or pipe without a trailing newline). This causes

11.reason: Accesses s[i-1] and s[i+1] without checking if i is at the beginning or end of the string. For a hyphen at position 0 or last valid index, these memory accesses are out-of-bounds, causing a potential runtime error.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Accesses s[i-1] and s[i+1] without checking if i is at the beginning or end of the string. For a hyphen at position 0 or last valid index, these memory accesses are out-of-bounds, causing a potential runtime error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Accessing s[i-1] when i=0 or s[i+1] when i is at the end of the string causes out-of-bounds memory access, leading to a runtime error. Bounds checking (i > 0 && s[i+1] != '\0') is required.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This condition accesses s[i-1] and s[i+1] without checking if i is at the start or end of the string, which can cause out-of-bounds access when i=0 or i points to the last character before '\n'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This condition accesses s[i+1] and s[i-1] without checking if i is at the start or end of the string, which can lead to out-of-bounds access when i=0 or i points to the last character before '\n'.]

13.reason: [Relies on unchecked s[i-1] and s[i+1]; also, the condition order may lead to reading invalid memory if i=0.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Relies on unchecked s[i-1] and s[i+1]; also, the condition order may lead to reading invalid memory if i=0.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Relies on unchecked s[i-1] and s[i+1]; if i=0, s[i-1] is invalid. Similarly, if s[i] is the last non-newline char, s[i+1] may be '\n' or beyond.]
